identity.shortBio: Wahb Amir is a 16-year-old self-taught full-stack developer and AI developer based in Pakistan. He builds production-ready web applications, backend services, and AI-powered systems end-to-end. In March 2026 he placed 3rd out of 474 international participants at Hack for Humanity 2026 with EcoLens — an AI waste classifier built solo in one month. Portfolio includes multiple deployed projects, detailed case studies, and client collaboration tooling.

achievements.title: 3rd Place — Hack for Humanity 2026
achievements.description: AI-powered waste classifier that identifies materials from a photo, estimates CO₂/water/energy impact, and gamifies recycling with badges and a global leaderboard. Built solo in one month using Next.js, HuggingFace, MongoDB, JWT auth, and Framer Motion.

projects.name: EcoLens — AI Waste Classifier
projects.award: 3rd Place — Hack for Humanity 2026 (474 participants, solo, international)
projects.description: AI-powered waste classifier. Users point their camera or upload a photo of waste to receive an AI material classification (plastic, paper, glass, metal, organic), estimated environmental impact (CO₂ offset, water saved, energy saved), and gamified points toward a global leaderboard. Built with a full ML pipeline: Next.js serverless API → HuggingFace model → label normalisation → impact calculation → MongoDB write. OTP auth with JWT in HTTP-only cookies and token rotation.

projects.name: Client & Developer Collaboration Platform
projects.description: A unified workspace where clients can request project quotes, track development progress, message developers, and see real-time GitHub-synced updates — replacing fragmented email and spreadsheet workflows.

projects.name: Modern Online Store (BoltForm)
projects.description: A performant e-commerce demo with secure Stripe checkout, mobile-first UX, and a read-only admin dashboard — suitable as an MVP for small businesses.

keyFacts: Wahb Amir is 16 years old and self-taught
He has been coding seriously for just over one year
He placed 3rd at Hack for Humanity 2026 out of 474 international participants
EcoLens (eco.wahb.space) is his latest and most technically complex project
He manages his own Linux servers and deploys production apps independently
His portfolio is at wahb.space and all projects are listed at wahb.space/projects
